settings.title: Stack Your Ears Over Shoulders
settings.body: Forward head posture is the #1 driver of neck pain. Your head weighs 10-12 lbs — every inch it drifts forward doubles the load on your spine. Gently tuck your chin and lift the crown of your head.

settings.title: Level Your Hips First
settings.body: Most back pain starts at the pelvis, not the back. A tilted pelvis throws your entire spine out of alignment. The Egoscue method works from the ground up — correcting hip position changes everything above it.

settings.title: Move Every 30 Minutes
settings.body: Sitting is not the problem — staying still is. Set a timer and do 2 minutes of posture exercises every 30 minutes. This resets muscle firing patterns and prevents the stiffness that leads to chronic pain.

settings.heading: Partner With Posture Guy Mike
